title: [lora_l2.c:476] Callback invoked with stale data if thread aborted
description:
What: The rx_thread() invokes the callback (line 477) with rx_buf data after releasing lora_mutex but before the callback returns. If k_thread_abort() is called on this thread during callback execution, the callback may not complete properly.

Why it matters: If stop() calls k_thread_abort() while the callback (lichen_l2_input) is executing, the callback may be:
1. Mid-way through net_pkt allocation
2. Holding rx_mutex (acquired at line 1656 of lichen_l2.c)
3. Have partially written to net_pkt

The thread abort leaves the system in inconsistent state. This is acknowledged in the code (stop() sets ABORTED state), but the actual cleanup path relies on best-effort mutex reinitialization which is UB.

Evidence:
```c
// rx_thread after mutex unlock
if (cb) {
    cb(rx_buf, ret, rssi, snr, cb_user_data);  // can be aborted here
}
```

This is a known architectural limitation documented extensively, so marking as P2 code smell.
close_reason: Fixed: Added SECURITY comment at callback invocation site (line 476-494) documenting k_thread_abort() interruption risk and the LORA_ABORTED/needs_reinit() recovery mechanism
